46	/-! ## §0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit
47	
48	Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`
49	(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`), built over a general single-universe
50	base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R₀ := Y.presheaf`. -/
51	
52	namespace PresheafOfModules
53	
54	open InternalHom Opposite
55	
56	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
57	
58	/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
59	(restricted) unit `restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_` are identified `R₀(X)`-linearly with `R₀(X)` itself,
60	via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
61	substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
62	at `1` (proved from `φ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
63	noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
64	    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
65	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
66	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
67	    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
68	        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
69	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
70	  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
71	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
72	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
73	  exact
74	    { toFun := fun φ =>
75	        evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
76	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
77	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => rfl
78	      map_smul' := fun c φ => by
79	        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c φ)
80	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
81	      invFun := fun r =>
82	        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
83	          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
84	      left_inv := fun φ => by
85	        ext Y
86	        dsimp only
87	        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
88	        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply φ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
89	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
90	        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
91	        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
92	        rfl
93	      right_inv := fun r => by
94	        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
95	            (restr X.unop
96	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
97	            (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
98	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
99	        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
100	        exact mul_one r }
101	
102	/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`,
103	assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
104	`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = 𝟙_`). -/
105	noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
106	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := R₀)
107	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
108	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
109	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
110	    (fun {X Y} f => by
111	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun φ => ?_)
112	      change evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
113	            ((PresheafOfModules.dual
114	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
115	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y : Type u))
116	          = ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
117	              (evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
118	                (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)))
119	      have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
120	        (φ : restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
121	          restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
122	        (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
123	        (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
124	      have hrm : (restr X.unop
125	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
126	          (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
127	          = (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f := rfl
128	      rw [hrm] at key
129	      erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at key
130	      have hAB : (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)) : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ) = op (Over.mk f.unop) :=
131	        congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))
132	      have homAppHEq : ∀ {A B : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ} (_ : A = B), HEq (φ.app A) (φ.app B) := by
133	        intro A B h; subst h; rfl
134	      have hdt : evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
135	          ((PresheafOfModules.dual
136	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
137	          = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
138	        congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (homAppHEq hAB))
139	      exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key)
140	
141	end PresheafOfModules

149	/-! ## §A. The C-bridge: restriction commutes with the sheaf-level dual -/
150	
151	/-- **Restriction along an open immersion commutes with the sheaf-level dual (C-bridge).**
152	
153	Blueprint `lem:dual_restrict_iso` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_bridge`).  For an open immersion
154	`f : Y ⟶ X` and `M : X.Modules`, there is a canonical isomorphism of `𝒪_Y`-modules
155	```
156	  (dual M).restrict f  ≅  dual (M.restrict f)
157	```
158	natural in `M`, between the restriction of the sheaf-level dual and the dual of the
159	restriction.
160	
161	/- Planner strategy:
162	   Blueprint label: lem:dual_restrict_iso (~L5374).
163	
164	   Proof-sketch (blueprint §5.4):
165	   The proof runs at the PRESHEAF-OF-MODULES level (Step 3 of the tensorObj_restrict_iso
166	   H1∘H2 recipe already strips the outer sheafification).  Three ingredients:
167	
168	   (a) Per-V slice equivalence: for each V ≤ U (= image of f), the opens functor
169	       `f.opensFunctor` is fully faithful with image = {W ≤ U}, so
170	       `Over_Y V ≃ Over_X (f.opensFunctor V)`.  This is the per-open shadow of
171	       `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence` (CLOSED in Vestigial.lean via
172	       `overSliceSheafEquiv`).
173	
174	   (b) Agreement of codomain: the structure sheaf of Y agrees with that of X under (a).
175	
176	   (c) Ring-iso transport of module structure:
177	       `lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv` (CLOSED in PresheafInternalHom.lean as
178	       `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`):
179	         `RingEquiv e → Dual(restrictScalars e.toRingHom A) ≃ restrictScalars e.toRingHom (Dual A)`
180	       applies sectionwise at each V to transport the `𝒪_X(fV)`-module structure on
181	       `(dual M)|_f(V)` to the `𝒪_Y(V)`-module structure via the ring iso
182	       `β_V = (f.appIso V).inv : 𝒪_X(fV) ≅ 𝒪_Y(V)`.
183	
184	   High-level recipe (mirrors tensorObj_restrict_iso Steps 1–4 with `dual` in place of `⊗`):
185	   Step 1: `(Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (dual M)` — reduce `restrict`
186	           to abstract pullback.
187	   Step 2: `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom` — move pullback
188	           inside sheafification.
189	   Step 3: strip the outer sheafification via `(sheafification …).mapIso`.
190	   Step 4 (the genuine new build):  close the residual presheaf goal
191	             `pushforward β (PresheafOfModules.dual M.val)
192	                 ≅ PresheafOfModules.dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)`
193	           The ROUTE: sectionwise, at each V ≤ U, the value of the LHS is
194	           `Hom_{Over_X(fV)}(restr(fV) M.val, restr(fV) 𝟙_X)` and the value of the RHS is
195	           `Hom_{Over_Y V}(restr V (pushforward β M.val), restr V 𝟙_Y)`.
196	           The slice equivalence (a) identifies these indexing categories; the agreement (b)
197	           identifies the codomain `𝟙`; the ring-iso transport (c) via

199	           Naturality in V is automatic on the thin poset `Opens X` by `Subsingleton.elim`.
200	
201	   ⚠ WARM-CONTEXT WARNING (progress-critic pc251):
202	   The residual presheaf goal (Step 4) is a GENUINE NEW BUILD — NOT covered by

230	noncomputable def dual_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
231	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M : X.Modules) :
232	    (dual M).restrict f ≅ dual (M.restrict f) := by
233	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`.
234	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (dual M) ≪≫ ?_
235	  -- Step 2. Sheafification commutes with pullback.
236	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
237	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) ≪≫ ?_
238	  -- Step 3. Strip the outer sheafification, descending to the presheaf residual.
239	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
240	      (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
241	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL): the presheaf goal
242	  --   `(pullback φ).obj (dual M.val) ≅ dual ((M.restrict f).val)`.
243	  -- H1: replace `pullback φ` with `pushforward β` (β the open-immersion structure ring iso).
244	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
245	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
246	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
247	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
248	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
249	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
250	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
251	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
252	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
253	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
254	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_

258	/-! ## §B. Local triviality of the dual -/
259	
260	/-- **Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit.**
261	`PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ = ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`, the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism.
262	Local supplement (the `PresheafOfModules`-level ingredient of `dual_unit_iso`). -/
263	noncomputable def presheafDualUnitIso {Y : Scheme.{u}} :
264	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := Y.presheaf)
265	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
266	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
267	  PresheafOfModules.dualUnitIsoGen (R₀ := Y.presheaf)
268	
269	/-- **The dual of the structure sheaf is the structure sheaf.** `dual 𝒪_Y ≅ 𝒪_Y`.
270	The presheaf-level dual of the monoidal unit `𝟙_` is the unit (evaluation at `1`),
271	sheafified and identified with the (already-sheaf) unit by the sheafification counit.
272	Mirrors `tensorObj_unit_iso` with the presheaf left unitor replaced by
273	`presheafDualUnitIso`. The third leg of the `dual_isLocallyTrivial` chain. -/
274	noncomputable def dual_unit_iso {Y : Scheme.{u}} :
275	    dual (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
276	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
277	      (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := Y)) ≪≫
278	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
279	      (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)

281	/-- **The dual of a locally-trivial `𝒪_X`-module is locally trivial.**

284	`LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial L`, then `dual L` is also locally trivial.

332	lemma dual_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {L : X.Modules}
333	    (hL : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial L) :
334	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (dual L) := by
335	  -- Mirrors `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`: trivialise the dual on each affine open `U`
336	  -- where `L` is trivial, via the three-step chain
337	  --   `(dual L)|_U ≅ dual (L|_U) ≅ dual 𝒪_U ≅ 𝒪_U`.
338	  intro x
339	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eL⟩⟩ := hL x

345	open Opposite TopologicalSpace in
346	/-- **The local section of the hom-sheaf manufactured from `f i`** (the load-bearing piece
347	of `homOfLocalCompat`, blueprint `localSection`).  Working with the underlying `Ab`-presheaves
348	`F = M.val.presheaf`, `G = N.val.presheaf`, the presheaf of types
349	`presheafHom F G` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom`) sends an open `W` to the morphisms of
350	the restrictions of `F`, `G` to the slice `Over W`.  Its value at `U i` is built from the
351	components of `f i`, conjugated by `eqToHom` along the down-set identity
352	`(U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V` (valid for `V ≤ U i`).  The naturality field — the genuine
353	coherence risk — is automatic on the thin poset `Opens X` once the `eqToHom`-conjugation is
354	peeled, via `Subsingleton.elim` on the hom-sets. -/
355	noncomputable def homLocalSection {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} {ι : Type*}
356	    (U : ι → X.Opens) (f : ∀ i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι) (i : ι) :
357	    (CategoryTheory.presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf).obj (op (U i)) where
358	  app W :=
359	    haveI hle : W.unop.left ≤ U i := W.unop.hom.le
360	    haveI himg : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left) = W.unop.left := by
361	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
362	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr hle
363	    M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg.symm)) ≫
364	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left)) ≫
365	      N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg))
366	  naturality := by
367	    intro A B φ
368	    have hBA : (unop B).left ≤ (unop A).left := ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).le
369	    let κ : (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left ⟶ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left :=
370	      (Opens.map (U i).ι.base).map (homOfLE hBA)
371	    have himgA : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left) = (unop A).left := by
372	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
373	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop A).hom.le
374	    have himgB : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left) = (unop B).left := by
375	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
376	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop B).hom.le
377	    -- naturality of the underlying ab-presheaf morphism of `f i`
378	    have hm := ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).naturality κ.op
379	    -- the two thin-poset square edges agree (`Opens X` is a thin poset)
380	    have hsubM : ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op ≫ eqToHom (congrArg op himgB.symm)
381	        = eqToHom (congrArg op himgA.symm) ≫ ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op :=
382	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
383	    have hsubN : ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op ≫ eqToHom (congrArg op himgB)
384	        = eqToHom (congrArg op himgA) ≫ ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op :=
385	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
386	    -- M-side: the φ-restriction followed by the `eqToHom` is the `eqToHom` followed by `κ`
387	    have hML : M.val.presheaf.map ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op ≫
388	          M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgB.symm))
389	        = M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgA.symm)) ≫
390	          (M.restrict (U i).ι).val.presheaf.map κ.op := by
391	      rw [(M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubM]
392	      exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _
393	    -- N-side analogue
394	    have hNR : N.val.presheaf.map ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op ≫
395	          N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgB))
396	        = N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgA)) ≫
397	          N.val.presheaf.map ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op := by
398	      rw [(N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubN]
399	      exact N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _
400	    dsimp only [Functor.comp_map, Functor.op_map, Functor.op_obj, Functor.comp_obj]
401	    rw [← Category.assoc, hML]
402	    erw [Category.assoc, reassoc_of% hm, hNR]
403	    simp only [Category.assoc]
404	    rfl

407	/-- **Convert a section of `presheafHom F G` over the terminal open `⊤` into a global
408	morphism `F ⟶ G`.**  Since `⊤` is terminal in `Opens X`, the value of `presheafHom F G`
409	at `op ⊤` already determines a full compatible family of sections (each open's value is the
410	restriction of the top section), which `presheafHomSectionsEquiv` identifies with a morphism

413	    {F G : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}}
414	    (s : (CategoryTheory.presheafHom F G).obj (op ⊤)) : F ⟶ G :=
415	  CategoryTheory.presheafHomSectionsEquiv F G
416	    ⟨fun W => (CategoryTheory.presheafHom F G).map (homOfLE le_top).op s, by
417	      intro W W' e
418	      dsimp only
419	      rw [← Functor.map_comp_apply]
420	      congr 1⟩

424	evaluates to the `Over.mk (homOfLE le_top)`-component of the top section `s`. -/
425	lemma topSectionToHom_app {X : TopCat.{u}}

427	    (s : (CategoryTheory.presheafHom F G).obj (op ⊤)) (W : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) :
428	    (topSectionToHom s).app W = s.app (op (Over.mk (homOfLE (le_top) : W.unop ⟶ ⊤))) := by
429	  obtain ⟨W⟩ := W
430	  exact CategoryTheory.presheafHom_map_app_op_mk_id (homOfLE le_top) s

432	/-- **A-bridge: compatible local `𝒪_X`-module morphisms glue to a global morphism.**
433	
434	Blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` (~L5592).  Let `X` be a scheme,
435	`M N : X.Modules`, and `{U i}` an indexed open cover of `X`.  If for each `i` we have a
436	morphism `f i : M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι` in `Scheme.Modules (U i)` such
437	that for all `i j` the restrictions of `f i` and `f j` to `U i ⊓ U j` are equal (as
438	morphisms in `Scheme.Modules (U i ⊓ U j)`, compared via `HEq` since the double-restriction
439	routes give propositionally but not definitionally equal source/target objects), then there
440	is a unique global morphism `M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules` whose restriction to each `U i` is `f i`.
441	
442	The compatibility hypothesis `hf` uses `HEq` (heterogeneous equality) to compare the two
443	restricted morphisms: the LHS `(restrictFunctor (resLE i)).map (f i)` has source
444	`(M.restrict (U i).ι).restrict (resLE i)` and the RHS has source
445	`(M.restrict (U j).ι).restrict (resLE j)`, which are propositionally equal (both equal
446	`M.restrict (U i ⊓ U j).ι` via `restrictFunctorComp`+`restrictFunctorCongr`) but not
447	definitionally equal.  The prover should establish `HEq` by first proving the types equal
448	via `congr`+`restrictFunctorComp`, then `heq_of_eq`.

10	**iter-254: the iter-253 `homOfLocalCompat` block was a SELF-IMPOSED throttle, not a real freeze.**
11	
12	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homOfLocalCompat` (in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean`) carries
13	an in-file comment claiming its `hf` hypothesis is "PROTECTED, frozen for the prover." This is **FALSE**:
14	- `archon-protected.yaml` lists ONLY decls in `Genus.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`, `AbelJacobi.lean`.
15	  `homOfLocalCompat` is ABSENT (verified first-hand iter-254).
16	- It has **no compiling caller** (grep: its only consumer `exists_tensorObj_inverse` in
17	  `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` is itself a `sorry`).
18	
19	So the prover MAY and SHOULD re-sign `hf`. The current `hf : ∀ i j, HEq ((pullback (resLE …)).map (f i))
20	((pullback (resLE …)).map (f j))` is **unsatisfiable** — the two `Scheme.Modules.pullback`-images live in
21	sheafifications of pullback presheaves along DIFFERENT morphisms, only isomorphic (not propositionally
22	equal), so every `HEq`-elimination (`eq_of_heq`/`HEq.elim`/`conj_eqToHom_iff_heq`/`subst`) fails and no
23	caller can produce the datum.
24	
25	**FIX**: re-sign `hf` to the honest **sectionwise** form — for all `i j` and open `V ≤ Uᵢ ⊓ Uⱼ`, the section
26	maps `(f i).val.app(V)` and `(f j).val.app(V)` agree in the fixed `Ab` hom-type `M.val(V) ⟶ N.val(V)`. Then
27	sub-step (a) `IsCompatible` closes DIRECTLY (the iter-253 `simp only [homLocalSection]` reduction IS exactly
28	this equation; the thin-poset route-difference collapses by `Subsingleton.elim` — its only legitimate use,
29	NOT to bridge the section maps). Blueprint sub-step (a) of `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` corrected
30	to this form (bw254); br254 confirmed sound + satisfiable. pc254: the close is SOUND, no residual gap.
31	
32	LESSON: when a prover reports a signature as "PROTECTED," VERIFY against `archon-protected.yaml` directly — a
33	stale in-file comment is not the source of truth. `homOfLocalCompat` is the frontier-base A-bridge that
34	`exists_tensorObj_inverse` (→ RPF group inverse) consumes. Related: [[ts254-delta-mu-spelling-fix]].

80	### The prescription (tscmp254 — read `analogies/tscmp254.md` IN FULL first)
81	The square is a **monoidal-functor comparison naturality in disguise.** Recast it onto the **two
82	single-sided naturality lemmas** of the sheafification monoidal functor — the SAME functor S2 already
83	uses (`Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ_natural` at ~L2016). Each single-sided lemma whiskers on ONE side against
84	ONE `μ`/`δ`, so **every term stays in a single `MonoidalCategoryStruct` instance — the cross-group
85	crossing never forms and `tensorHom_def` is never needed.** This is exactly how Mathlib makes
86	sheafification monoidal (`Sheaf.monoidalCategory`/`LocalizedMonoidal` read `μ_natural_left :=
87	NatTrans.naturality_app …` off a bifunctor iso, never `whisker_exchange`). Do NOT instantiate
88	`LocalizedMonoidal` literally (needs `MonoidalClosed` + `J.W.IsMonoidal`, DEAD per memory ts233) — borrow
89	only the principle via the δ/μ lemmas.
90	
91	Three coordinated moves:
92	
93	1. **STEP A — recast `sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_natural` (~L1914) via `δ_natural_left`/`δ_natural_right`**
94	   (or `μ_natural_left`/`μ_natural_right` — pick the variance matching `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`). [verified]
95	   `Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ_natural_left`/`δ_natural_right` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Functor.lean:246,250`,
96	   both `@[reassoc (attr := simp)]`); `μ_natural_left`/`μ_natural_right` (`:70,74`, `@[reassoc (attr := simp)]`).
97	   **Least-invasive first:** try proving the naturality by rewriting with the single-sided δ/μ lemmas
98	   directly (the goal's η-whiskers ARE the `μ`/`δ` of `sheafification ⋙ forget`), WITHOUT changing the
99	   def of `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` — if `sheafifyTensorUnitIso.hom` is already defeq to a composite of one
100	   `δ`/`μ` whiskered on each side, the two `_left`/`_right` lemmas close it.
101	   Only **if** that is blocked: restate `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` so it is *literally* the comparison
102	   `δ`/`μ` of `sheafification ⋙ forget`, then the lemmas apply by construction. If you restate it, keep
103	   D2′ (`pullbackTensorMap_unit_isIso`, CLOSED) GREEN — it consumes `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`.
104	
105	2. **STEP A/B spelling pin — state `pullbackTensorMap` and its helper isos on the SINGLE canonical
106	   ring-functor spelling** `X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` (where the monoidal instance is
107	   registered: `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean:32,104-105`), NOT
108	   `Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf` nor `X.ringCatSheaf.obj`. This is the root of BOTH the STEP-A instance
109	   collision AND the STEP-B Square-2 `Sheaf.val`/`.obj` `Functor.comp_map` merge (~L2006–2027) — pinning
110	   the spelling clears both. Make the minimal change that removes the `letI instMS` bridges; do not
111	   gratuitously restate decls D2′ depends on unless required (keep the file green at each checkpoint).
112	
113	3. **Strip `restrictScalars (𝟙)` EAGERLY** with `rw [restrictScalarsId_map]` (the project's own lemma —
114	   Mathlib has no presheaf-level `restrictScalarsId` NatIso; the ModuleCat-level analog is [verified]
115	   `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsId'App_inv_naturality`, `ChangeOfRings.lean:217`) **BEFORE** any whisker/erw
116	   step, so the `whnf` from the dead approach #2 is never triggered. The wrapper originates at
117	   `Mathlib/.../Presheaf/Sheafify.lean:329` (`toSheafify` bakes `restrictScalars α` into the unit codomain).
118	
119	### STEP B — close D1′ `pullbackTensorMap_natural` (~L2027)
120	Once STEP A closes and the spelling is pinned: the 4-square paste (iter-253 task result + corrected
121	blueprint D1′ sketch). Square-1 (`sheafificationCompPullback` naturality) already CLOSED; Square-2 merge
122	dissolved by the canonical spelling (move 2); S2 = `δ_natural`; S3 = the now-closed STEP A; S4 =
123	`pullbackValIso_hom_natural` (CLOSED). **Attempt BOTH STEP A and STEP B this session** (pc254: same root
124	fix unblocks both; don't stop at STEP A). If STEP A consumes the budget, leave STEP B scaffolded with a
125	one-line handoff naming the next spelling-normalization step.
126	
127	### Bars / signals / guardrails
128	**Bar:** close STEP A (the δ/μ recast) + D1′ at minimum. D3′/D4′ NOT this iter (gated on D1′).
129	**HARD STOPPING CONDITION (pc254):** `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` MUST be left COMPILING at session end
130	(DualInverse imports it). If STEP A does not close, restore its `sorry` and leave the file GREEN before
131	stopping — never leave a mid-edit broken build.
132	**REVERSING SIGNAL:** if the δ/μ recast (move 1) does NOT close STEP A — do NOT retry any of the 3 banned
133	approaches, do NOT invent a 4th tactic pivot. STOP, leave a compiling partial, and report the EXACT
134	residual goal + which δ/μ lemma you applied + where it failed. The planner takes the next structural
135	decision next iter (a `refactor`-subagent restatement of the defs onto the canonical spelling, or
136	reordering to finish the dual chain first) — this is autonomous, no user escalation.
137	**Guardrails:** do NOT touch `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (~L708). Do NOT revive the general Lan build.
138	**Minor cleanup (you own this file):** stale `(iter-252)` header label (~L43) + `~L699` line ref (actual
139	L708) → fix; the stale `// Next iter:` annotation in the STEP-A residual → drop once STEP A closes.
140

5925:        lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat, lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict,
5941:  \cref{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat} --- to a single global morphism

5768	  \label{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}
5769	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homOfLocalCompat}
5770	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and \(\{U_i\}\) an
5771	  open cover of \(X\). Suppose given, for each \(i\), a morphism
5772	  \(f_i \colon M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,U_i\) such that
5773	  for all \(i, j\) the restrictions of \(f_i\) and \(f_j\) to \(U_i \cap U_j\) agree.
5774	  Then there is a unique global morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in
5775	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) whose restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\).
5776	\end{lemma}
5777	
5778	\begin{proof}
5779	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_homMk, lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}
5780	  A morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is, concretely, a morphism of the
5781	  underlying sheaves of abelian groups that is in addition \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear
5782	  sectionwise. We build \(f\) in two steps.
5783	
5784	  \emph{(i) Glue the underlying ab-sheaf morphism.} Forget \(M\) and \(N\) to their
5785	  underlying sheaves of abelian groups. The morphisms-between-them presheaf
5786	  \(\mathcal{H}\) (\(\mathtt{presheafHom}\) of the underlying presheaves of abelian groups
5787	  of \(M\) and \(N\)), whose value at an open \(W\) is the abelian
5788	  group of morphisms \(M|_W \to N|_W\) of presheaves of abelian groups, is itself a
5789	  \emph{sheaf of types} --- this is the ``hom into a sheaf is a sheaf'' principle
5790	  \(\mathtt{Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom}\), which consumes the sheaf condition of \(N\).
5791	  Regarding \(\mathcal{H}\) as a \(\mathtt{TopCat.Sheaf}\), its universal gluing
5792	  property \(\mathtt{existsUnique\_gluing}\) amalgamates any compatible family of local
5793	  sections of \(\mathcal{H}\) into a unique global section. This route is preferred over
5794	  the raw sieve-level amalgamation \(\mathtt{presheafHom\_isSheafFor}\), which carries
5795	  strictly more bookkeeping.
5796	
5797	  The load-bearing datum is, for each \(i\), the \emph{local section}
5798	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i \in \mathcal{H}(U_i)\)
5799	  of \cref{lem:scheme_modules_hom_local_section},
5800	  manufactured from \(f_i\). Its component at a pair \((V, h : V \to U_i)\) is the given
5801	  component of \(f_i\), conjugated by the canonical \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\) identifications
5802	  that arise from the open-set equality \(\iota_i\bigl(\iota_i^{-1}(V)\bigr) = V\) valid
5803	  for \(V \subseteq U_i\) (the image under the open immersion \(\iota_i : U_i \to X\) of
5804	  the preimage of \(V\) is \(V\) again). The genuine coherence risk of the whole
5805	  construction is concentrated in the \emph{naturality condition} of
5806	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i\): the requirement that these
5807	  \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-conjugated components of \(f_i\) commute across the morphisms of
5808	  the slice category \(\mathtt{Over}\,U_i\). This is the one sub-piece to be
5809	  built and verified first, as a standalone lemma.
5810	
5811	  This \(\mathtt{localSection}\) naturality is precisely the \emph{section-direction
5812	  slice} of the comparison packaged in \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}:
5813	  converting the local datum \(f_i\), which lives over the open-immersion pullback
5814	  \(\mathtt{Opens}\,\widetilde{U_i}\), into a section of \(\mathcal{H}\) indexed over the
5815	  slice \(\mathtt{Over}\,U_i\) is the one-sided realisation of the open-immersion
5816	  \(\leftrightarrow\) slice transport. As there, the \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-conjugation is
5817	  controlled by the down-set identity \(\iota_i(\iota_i^{-1}(V)) = V\) for
5818	  \(V \subseteq U_i\), and the coherence is automatic on the thin poset
5819	  \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\) by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\). This A-engine uses the
5820	  sheaf-site equivalence \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}; the C-bridge
5821	  \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}, by contrast, uses a per-open slice comparison at
5822	  the presheaf level over the \emph{varying} section ring \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), and so
5823	  does \emph{not} reduce to the same sheaf-site root --- the two are independent and
5824	  must be built separately.
5825	
5826	  Of the remaining four sub-steps, three are mechanical and one --- the cocycle bridge
5827	  (a) --- carries the genuine content. We spell it out.
5828	
5829	  \emph{(a) The cocycle/\(\mathtt{IsCompatible}\) condition on the family
5830	  \((\mathtt{localSection}\,i)_i\).} The condition \(\mathtt{IsCompatible}\) asks that for
5831	  every pair of indices \(i, j\) the two restrictions of \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i\) and
5832	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,j\) to the overlap coincide as sections of the hom-sheaf
5833	  \(\mathcal{H}\) over \(U_i \sqcap U_j\), i.e.\ an honest equality in the single abelian
5834	  group \(\mathcal{H}(U_i \sqcap U_j)\). We must therefore identify the overlap-agreement
5835	  hypothesis on the family \((f_i)_i\) that furnishes this. The correct hypothesis --- and
5836	  the only one a caller can actually produce --- is the \emph{sectionwise} one:
5837	
5838	  \begin{quote}
5839	    for all indices \(i, j\) and every open \(V\) with \(V \subseteq U_i\) and
5840	    \(V \subseteq U_j\), the two section maps \((f_i).\mathtt{app}(V)\) and
5841	    \((f_j).\mathtt{app}(V)\) agree as morphisms in the single, fixed abelian-group hom-type
5842	    \(M(V) \to N(V)\), the value of the hom-presheaf \(\mathcal{H} = \mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) at
5843	    \(V\).
5844	  \end{quote}
5845	
5846	  This is exactly the data a caller has at hand: two local morphisms that literally agree on
5847	  every open contained in both \(U_i\) and \(U_j\).
5848	
5849	  It is worth contrasting this with a naive alternative that does \emph{not} work. One might
5850	  try to compare the two double-restrictions \((f_i)|_{U_i \cap U_j}\) and
5851	  \((f_j)|_{U_i \cap U_j}\) directly, as morphisms reached by the two slice-restriction
5852	  routes. But these two morphisms do not live in a common type at all: each is the image of
5853	  \(f\) under the pullback-of-modules functor along a \emph{different} slice-restriction
5854	  map, and the resulting source and target objects are sheafifications of pullback presheaves
5855	  along different morphisms. Such objects are only \emph{isomorphic}, through a nontrivial
5856	  comparison isomorphism, and are \emph{not} propositionally equal. A heterogeneous
5857	  comparison of the two double-restrictions therefore cannot be reduced to an honest
5858	  equation --- there is no object equality to transport along, so no
5859	  \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\) of the thin poset and no heterogeneous-to-homogeneous
5860	  elimination applies --- and, crucially, no caller can produce such a datum in the first
5861	  place. The sectionwise hypothesis sidesteps this entirely by comparing only the section
5862	  maps, which live in the fixed group \(M(V) \to N(V)\).
5863	
5864	  With the sectionwise hypothesis in hand the passage to \(\mathtt{IsCompatible}\) is
5865	  \emph{direct}. Restrict \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i\) to the overlap \(U_i \sqcap U_j\). By
5866	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_hom_local_section}, the component of \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i\)
5867	  at an open \(V \subseteq U_i\) is precisely \((f_i).\mathtt{app}(V')\), where
5868	  \(V' = \iota_i^{-1}(V)\), conjugated by the canonical \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\) identifications
5869	  arising from the down-set identity \(\iota_i\bigl(\iota_i^{-1}(V)\bigr) = V\); restricting
5870	  to the overlap simply evaluates this component at the opens \(V \subseteq U_i \sqcap U_j\),
5871	  and likewise for \(j\). Thus both restrictions, evaluated at such a \(V\), are conjugates
5872	  of \((f_i).\mathtt{app}(V')\) and \((f_j).\mathtt{app}(V')\) by \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)
5873	  morphisms, all lying in the fixed group \(M(V) \to N(V)\). The sectionwise hypothesis
5874	  equates the two middle terms \((f_i).\mathtt{app}\) and \((f_j).\mathtt{app}\) directly.
5875	  The only remaining identification is that the two \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-conjugation routes
5876	  --- built from the two down-set identities, one for \(U_i\) and one for \(U_j\) --- agree;
5877	  since both are parallel morphisms in the thin poset \((\mathtt{Opens}\,X)^{\mathrm{op}}\),
5878	  they coincide by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\). Hence the two restricted local sections
5879	  agree in \(\mathcal{H}(U_i \sqcap U_j)\), which is exactly \(\mathtt{IsCompatible}\).
5880	
5881	  Note the division of labour: the substantive content is the sectionwise agreement of the
5882	  \(f_i\), an equation in fixed abelian-group hom-types that a caller can supply; the
5883	  \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-conjugation and the \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\) collapse of the
5884	  thin-poset routes are the only mechanical identifications, and they are legitimate
5885	  precisely because they act on the thin poset of opens of \(X\), never on the genuinely
5886	  distinct \(\mathtt{Ab}\)-hom-set section maps.
5887	
5888	  The other three sub-steps are mechanical: (b) gluing via
5889	  \(\mathtt{existsUnique\_gluing}\) and converting the amalgamated section over
5890	  \(\mathrm{op}\,\top\) into a genuine morphism \(g\) of the underlying presheaves of
5891	  \(M\) and \(N\) --- the
5892	  \(\top\)-section-to-morphism passage being the global-sections equivalence
5893	  \(\mathtt{presheafHomSectionsEquiv}\) / \(\mathtt{sheafHomSectionsEquiv}\) of the
5894	  hom-sheaf \(\mathcal{H}\) (which, since \(\top\) is terminal in \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\),
5895	  lands the glued section as an honest morphism of presheaves), \emph{not} a hand-unfold
5896	  of the \(\top\)-section; (c) the
5897	  sectionwise linearity check; and (d) the recovery that the restriction of \(g\) to
5898	  each \(U_i\) is the ab-sheaf morphism underlying \(f_i\).
5899	
5900	  \emph{(ii) Promote to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear.} The linearity equation
5901	  \(g(r \cdot m) = r \cdot g(m)\) is a sectionwise identity between two sections of a
5902	  separated presheaf. It holds on each \(U_i\), because there \(g\) restricts to the
5903	  module morphism \(f_i\); since the two sides agree on the cover \(\{U_i\}\) and the
5904	  ambient presheaf is separated, they agree globally. Packaging \(g\) together with this
5905	  global linearity through the scheme-level wrapper \cref{def:scheme_modules_homMk}
5906	  (\(\mathtt{homMk}\)) yields a morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in
5907	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\); its restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\) and its
5908	  uniqueness are both inherited from the ab-sheaf amalgamation of step (i).
5909	
5910	  This gluing engine is substantially larger than a routine glue: it is dominated by the
5911	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\) naturality condition and the convert-and-recover bookkeeping, and
5912	  the naturality is the section-direction slice of
5913	  \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv} --- so its true cost is bounded below by
5914	  building that sheaf-site equivalence, not by the surrounding assembly. It is therefore to be
5915	  scoped as a multi-piece build --- with \(\mathtt{localSection}\), together with its
5916	  naturality, built as a standalone verified lemma first --- rather than a one-shot.
5917	  The step computes no tensor stalk --- it is purely a statement about gluing morphisms
5918	  of sheaves --- and so it does not re-enter the stalk-tensor commutation.
5919	\end{proof}
5920	
5921	\begin{remark}
5922	[How the dual infrastructure discharges the \(\otimes\)-inverse]
5923	  \label{rem:dual_discharges_inverse}
5924	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial,
5925	        lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat, lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict,
5926	        lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}

595	morphism.** Given a morphism `g : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf` of the underlying
596	`Ab`-presheaves that is sectionwise `𝒪_X`-linear, package it as a morphism `M ⟶ N`
597	of `𝒪_X`-modules. This wraps `PresheafOfModules.homMk` at the `Scheme.Modules` level;
598	it is the "promote to `𝒪_X`-linear" half of the descent A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`
599	(the ab-sheaf gluing produces the linear `g`; this lemma turns it into a module map).
600	Sectionwise linearity is a property the consumer checks on a separated presheaf. -/
601	noncomputable def homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
602	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
603	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
604	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
605	    M ⟶ N :=
606	  ⟨PresheafOfModules.homMk (M₁ := M.val) (M₂ := N.val) g hg⟩
607	
608	/-- The underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `homMk g hg` is `g` itself. -/
609	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map_homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
610	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
611	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
612	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
613	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map (homMk g hg) = g :=
614	  rfl

432	/-- **A-bridge: compatible local `𝒪_X`-module morphisms glue to a global morphism.**
433	
434	Blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` (~L5592).  Let `X` be a scheme,
435	`M N : X.Modules`, and `{U i}` an indexed open cover of `X`.  If for each `i` we have a
436	morphism `f i : M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι` in `Scheme.Modules (U i)` such
437	that for all `i j` the restrictions of `f i` and `f j` to `U i ⊓ U j` are equal (as
438	morphisms in `Scheme.Modules (U i ⊓ U j)`, compared via `HEq` since the double-restriction
439	routes give propositionally but not definitionally equal source/target objects), then there
440	is a unique global morphism `M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules` whose restriction to each `U i` is `f i`.
441	
442	The compatibility hypothesis `hf` uses `HEq` (heterogeneous equality) to compare the two
443	restricted morphisms: the LHS `(restrictFunctor (resLE i)).map (f i)` has source
444	`(M.restrict (U i).ι).restrict (resLE i)` and the RHS has source
445	`(M.restrict (U j).ι).restrict (resLE j)`, which are propositionally equal (both equal
446	`M.restrict (U i ⊓ U j).ι` via `restrictFunctorComp`+`restrictFunctorCongr`) but not
447	definitionally equal.  The prover should establish `HEq` by first proving the types equal
448	via `congr`+`restrictFunctorComp`, then `heq_of_eq`.

451	   Blueprint label: lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat (~L5592).
452	   Uses (all CLOSED):

455	                                            (TensorObjSubstrate/Vestigial.lean ~L715)
456

458	
459	   Step (i) — Glue the underlying ab-sheaf morphism:
460	   Forget M, N to their underlying sheaves of abelian groups.  The presheaf
461	   `H(W) = Hom_{Ab-preshvs}(M.val.presheaf|_W, N.val.presheaf|_W)` is a sheaf of TYPES:
462	   this is `Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom` (Mathlib), consuming the sheaf condition of N.
463	   Convert each `f i` to a local section `s i ∈ H(U i)` via the open-immersion slice
464	   transport `overSliceSheafEquiv` (Vestigial.lean):
465	     - `s i` at a pair `(V, h : V ≤ U i)` is `(f i).val.app` at the corresponding open of
466	       `(U i : Scheme)`, conjugated by `eqToHom` identifications from the down-set identity
467	       `ι_i(ι_i⁻¹(V)) = V` for `V ≤ U i`.  The naturality of `s i` in V is the
468	       section-direction slice of `overSliceSheafEquiv` and is automatic on the thin poset
469	       `Opens X` by `Subsingleton.elim`.
470	   Apply `TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing` (or `Presheaf.IsSheaf.existsUnique_gluing`) to
471	   amalgamate the compatible family `(s i)_i` into a unique global section
472	   `s ∈ H(⊤) = (M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)`.
473	   Convert the amalgamated `s` to an ab-presheaf morphism `g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf`
474	   via `presheafHomSectionsEquiv` / `sheafHomSectionsEquiv`.
475	
476	   Step (ii) — Promote to `𝒪_X`-linear via `homMk`:
477	   The linearity `g(r • m) = r • g(m)` holds on each `U i` (since `g|_{U i}` comes from
478	   the module morphism `f i`), and the two sides agree globally because the ambient presheaf
479	   is separated.  Apply `Scheme.Modules.homMk g (sectionwise-linearity proof)` to produce
480	   `M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`.
481	
482	   Key sub-lemma to build first (most fragile piece):
483	   The naturality field of `s i` — that the `eqToHom`-conjugated components of `f i` commute
484	   across morphisms of the slice `Over (U i)` — is dominated by `overSliceSheafEquiv` and
485	   should be extracted as a standalone axiom-clean lemma before the full gluing assembly.
486	
487	   Named CLOSED base lemmas:
488	   - `Scheme.Modules.homMk` (TensorObjSubstrate.lean ~L598).
489	   - `Vestigial.overSliceSheafEquiv` (TensorObjSubstrate/Vestigial.lean ~L715).
490	   - `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom` (Mathlib) — hom into a sheaf is a sheaf.
491	   - `TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing` (Mathlib) — gluing of compatible sections.
492	   - `presheafHomSectionsEquiv` / `sheafHomSectionsEquiv` (Mathlib) — top-section ↔ morphism.
493	
494	   Implementation note: this is a MULTI-PIECE BUILD dominated by the `s i` naturality field.
495	   Build `s i` (and its naturality) as a standalone verified lemma FIRST, before assembling
496	   the full gluing.  The step does NOT invoke any tensor stalk — it is purely about gluing
497	   morphisms of sheaves.

500	noncomputable def homOfLocalCompat {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
501	    {ι : Type*} (U : ι → X.Opens) (hU : ∀ x : X, ∃ i, x ∈ U i)
502	    (f : ∀ i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι)
503	    (hf : ∀ i j,
504	        HEq ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
505	                (Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) (U i) (U i ⊓ U j) inf_le_left)).map (f i))
506	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
507	                (Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) (U j) (U i ⊓ U j) inf_le_right)).map (f j))) :
508	    M ⟶ N := by

510	  -- `presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom`) is a
511	  -- sheaf of types because `N` is a sheaf (`Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom`, consuming `N.isSheaf`).

open Opposite TopologicalSpace in
/-- **Down-set image identity.**  For `V ≤ W` (opens of a scheme `X`), the image under the
open immersion `W.ι` of the preimage of `V` is `V` again: `W.ι ''ᵁ (W.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V`.  This is
the equality powering the `eqToHom`-conjugations in `homLocalSection`. -/
lemma image_preimage_of_le {X : Scheme.{u}} (W : X.Opens) {V : X.Opens} (hV : V ≤ W) :
    W.ι ''ᵁ (W.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V := by
  simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
  exact inf_eq_right.mpr hV

/-- **A-bridge: compatible local `𝒪_X`-module morphisms glue to a global morphism.**

Blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` (~L5592).  Let `X` be a scheme,
`M N : X.Modules`, and `{U i}` an indexed open cover of `X`.  If for each `i` we have a
morphism `f i : M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι` in `Scheme.Modules (U i)` such
that the underlying section maps of `f i` and `f j` agree, *sectionwise*, on every open
`V ≤ U i ⊓ U j` (each conjugated into the fixed abelian-group hom-type `M(V) ⟶ N(V)` by the
canonical `eqToHom`s from the down-set identity `ι(ι⁻¹V) = V`), then there is a unique global
morphism `M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules` whose restriction to each `U i` is `f i`.

The compatibility hypothesis `hf` is the **sectionwise** overlap-agreement (iter-254 re-sign;
this `def` is NOT in `archon-protected.yaml` and has no compiling caller, so the prover owns its
signature).  The earlier `HEq` form — comparing the two `Scheme.Modules.pullback`-images of
`f i`, `f j` along the two slice-restrictions — was *unsatisfiable*: those images live in
sheafifications of pullback presheaves along *different* morphisms, hence in only-isomorphic
(not propositionally equal) objects, so no `HEq`-elimination applies and no caller can produce
the datum.  The sectionwise form compares only the section maps, which live in the fixed group
`M(V) ⟶ N(V)`, and is exactly the data a caller (two local morphisms literally agreeing on
overlaps) has at hand.  See blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` sub-step (a).

open Opposite TopologicalSpace in
noncomputable def homOfLocalCompat {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
    {ι : Type*} (U : ι → X.Opens) (hU : ∀ x : X, ∃ i, x ∈ U i)
    (f : ∀ i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι)
    (hf : ∀ (i j : ι) (V : X.Opens) (hVi : V ≤ U i) (hVj : V ≤ U j),
        M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op (image_preimage_of_le (U i) hVi).symm)) ≫
          ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V)) ≫
            N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op (image_preimage_of_le (U i) hVi)))
          = M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op (image_preimage_of_le (U j) hVj).symm)) ≫
              ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f j).val).app (op ((U j).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V)) ≫
                N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op (image_preimage_of_le (U j) hVj)))) :
    M ⟶ N := by

attribute [local simp] pseudofunctor_associativity pseudofunctor_left_unitality
  pseudofunctor_right_unitality Bicategory.toNatTrans_conjugateEquiv
  conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom Adjunction.ofCat_comp conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv in
/-- The pseudofunctor from `Schemeᵒᵖ` to the bicategory of adjunctions which sends
a scheme `X` to the category `X.Modules` of sheaves of modules over `X`.
(This contains both the covariant and the contravariant functorialities of
these categories.) -/
@[simps! obj_obj map_l map_r map_adj
  mapId_hom_τl mapId_hom_τr mapId_inv_τl mapId_inv_τr
  mapComp_hom_τl mapComp_hom_τr mapComp_inv_τl mapComp_inv_τr]
def pseudofunctor :
    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))

end Functorial

noncomputable section Restriction

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]

/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
but has better defeqs. -/
def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
  (restrictFunctor f).obj M

@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_

input.new_string:
open Opposite TopologicalSpace in
/-- **Sectionwise `𝒪_X`-linearity of the local hom-section component.**  The component of
`homLocalSection U f i` at an open `W ≤ U i` is the `eqToHom`-conjugate of the module-morphism
section map `(f i).val.app`, hence `X.ringCatSheaf(W)`-linear: the two outer restriction maps are
`map_smul`-semilinear and the inner `(f i)`-component is linear over the structure ring of `U i`,
which the open immersion identifies with `X`'s structure ring over the image. -/
lemma homLocalSection_app_smul {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} {ι : Type*}
    (U : ι → X.Opens) (f : ∀ i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι) (i : ι)
    {W : X.Opens} (hW : W ≤ U i)
    (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (op W)) (m : M.val.obj (op W)) :
    ((homLocalSection U f i).app (op (Over.mk (homOfLE hW)))).hom (r • m)
      = r • ((homLocalSection U f i).app (op (Over.mk (homOfLE hW)))).hom m := by
  simp only [homLocalSection, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
  sorry

open Opposite TopologicalSpace in
/-- **A-bridge: compatible local `𝒪_X`-module morphisms glue to a global morphism.**

# Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean — iter-254 (Lane TS-inv)

Sorry count: **2 → 2** (declaration-level), but the structure is materially advanced:
`homOfLocalCompat` went from **2 internal sorries → 1**, with sub-step (a) fully closed and
sub-step (c) ~90% built. File compiles green (verified `lake build AlgebraicJacobian`, 8367 jobs).

## STEP A — `homOfLocalCompat` (re-sign `hf` + close)

### Re-sign `hf` (DONE)
Per objectives + memory [[ts254-homoflocalcompat-not-protected]]: `homOfLocalCompat` is NOT in
`archon-protected.yaml` and has no compiling caller, so I re-signed its `hf` hypothesis from the
unsatisfiable `HEq` form to the **sectionwise** form:
```
hf : ∀ (i j : ι) (V : X.Opens) (hVi : V ≤ U i) (hVj : V ≤ U j),
    M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op (image_preimage_of_le (U i) hVi).symm)) ≫
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V)) ≫
        N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op (image_preimage_of_le (U i) hVi)))
      = … (same with j) …
```
i.e. the two `f i`/`f j` section maps agree, conjugated into the fixed group `M(V) ⟶ N(V)`.
Added a small helper `image_preimage_of_le` (the down-set identity `W.ι ''ᵁ (W.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V`).
Updated the docstring (was contradicting the body, aud253 major #3).

### Sub-step (a) `IsCompatible` — **CLOSED**
After the iter-253 reduction (`presheafHom_map_app` ×2 + `simp only [homLocalSection]`), the goal is
exactly the sectionwise equation; closes by
`exact hf i j W.left (W.hom.le.trans inf_le_left) (W.hom.le.trans inf_le_right)`
(the `eqToHom`-conjugations and `(Over.mk (W.hom ≫ infLE_)).left ≡ W.left` match by **definitional
proof irrelevance** / defeq — no `Subsingleton.elim` needed for the section maps).

### Sub-step (c) `𝒪_X`-linearity `hg` — **reduced to one isolated ring-bridge sorry**
Built and verified (all compiling):
1. **Separatedness reduction** via `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.section_ext` (point-based): pick the cover
   member `U i ∋ x`, neighbourhood `W := V.unop ⊓ U i`.
2. **Naturality + `map_smul` reduction**: `erw [← NatTrans.naturality_apply g …, map_smul M.val …,
   map_smul N.val …, ← NatTrans.naturality_apply g …]` reduces the global linearity at `V` to local
   linearity of `g.app (op W)` at `W ≤ U i`.
3. **Connection lemma `hconn`** (the hard transport + gluing piece — fully proved): for `W' ≤ U i`,
   `g.app (op W') = (homLocalSection U f i).app (op (Over.mk (homOfLE _)))`. Proof: unfold `g` via
   `topSectionToHom_app`; transport `hsup ▸ s₀` to `H.obj.map (eqToHom …)` via a generic `subst`
   helper `htr`; rewrite `eqToHom (congrArg op hsup) = (eqToHom hsup.symm).op` by `Subsingleton.elim`
   (thin poset `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ`); apply `presheafHom_map_app` twice (once for the `hsup`-transport, once
   for the `IsGluing` datum `_hs`); identify the two `W' ⟶ iSup U` slice morphisms by
   `Subsingleton.elim`.
4. **Composite decomposition + M-leg `map_smul`**: `erw [ConcreteCategory.comp_apply ×4,
   PresheafOfModules.map_smul]` decomposes the triple composite
   `M.map (eqToHom e₁) ≫ (f i).val.app P ≫ N.map (eqToHom e₂)` and pushes the scalar through the M-leg.
   (Note: `rw`/`simp` with `comp_apply` do NOT fire — must use **`erw`** for the wrapped
   `AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom`.)

**Remaining (the single sorry at ~L636 inside `homOfLocalCompat`):** the genuine open-immersion
**ring-bridge**. The inner leg `((toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op P)` is, at the `Ab` level,
only additive; its linearity over `(U i).ringCatSheaf(P)` must be reinterpreted at the `(U i)`
`ModuleCat` level. The open immersion identifies `(U i).ringCatSheaf(P) ≅ X.ringCatSheaf((U i).ι ''ᵁ P)`
via the structure-ring iso `((U i).ι.appIso P)`, through which `restrictFunctor (U i).ι` defines the
`(U i)`-module structure on `M.restrict (U i).ι` as `restrictScalars` along `((U i).ι.appIso P).inv`
(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf.restrictFunctor`). The route (each ingredient confirmed to
exist):
- `((pushforward φ).obj X).obj X₁ = (restrictScalars (φ.app X₁)).obj …` (`PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_obj`),
  and `restrictScalars` smul is **`rfl`** (`r •_restrict x = (g r) •_orig x`, verified in scratch).

  `(f i).app (s •_{Ui} z) = s •_{Ui} (f i).app z`.
- Transport back + the N-leg `map_smul` + scalar reconciliation (`X.ring.map (eqToHom e₁)`,
  `X.ring.map (eqToHom e₂)` compose back to `id` on `W` since `e₂ = e₁.symm`).

defeq carrier; see the d.2/restrictScalars memories). It is now maximally isolated: everything around
